headline: Graco Inc. Announces Appointment of Sanjiv Gupta as Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer; David M. Lowe to Retire After Three Decades of Service
summary: MINNEAPOLIS, March 02, 2026--Graco Inc. (NYSE: GGG) announced today that it has appointed Sanjiv Gupta as Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, effective April 15, 2026. Gupta will succeed David M. Lowe in the role, who recently informed the company of his intention to retire after a more than thirty-year career with Graco.

headline: Dow Jones leads modest retreat as Wall Street aloof to Iran war
summary: 10am: Wall Street starts with modest falls Initial trading in New York was less pessimistic than in Europe, with losses for the main US indices considerably smaller in initial trades. The Dow Jones is the worst, down 0.65%, followed by the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both falling...

headline: Is General Motors (GM) Still A Value Play After Its 68% One Year Rally
summary: If you are wondering whether General Motors at around US$78.71 is still a value play or now pricing in a lot of optimism, you are not alone. Over the past year the stock has returned 67.8%, building on a 3 year return of 100.3% and a 5 year return of 48.4%. This performance comes even though the share price has seen a 1.2% decline over the last week, a 6.3% decline over the last month and a 2.8% decline year to date. Recent headlines around General Motors have kept investors focused on how...

headline: 3 Large-Cap Stocks We Find Risky
summary: Large-cap stocks usually command their industries because they have the scale to drive market trends. The flip side though is that their sheer size can limit growth as expanding further becomes an increasingly challenging task.

headline: The Obscure Fee Costing You More to Buy a Car
summary: It is called the “destination charge” and is now running an average $1,600.  Automakers have been upping the fee by hundreds of dollars in recent years, as fuel and shipping costs have risen.  Overall, car buyers spent more than $26 billion to cover destination charges in 2025, according to data from Edmunds, the online car-shopping resource.

headline: Does Iowa’s Data-Privacy Lawsuit Against OnStar Reframe the Bull Case For General Motors (GM)?
summary: In late February 2026, Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird filed a lawsuit against General Motors and OnStar, alleging they collected and sold Iowans’ detailed driving data without informed consent, in violation of the Iowa Consumer Fraud Act. The case spotlights how GM’s push to monetize connected-vehicle services via programs like OnStar Smart Driver can create material legal, regulatory, and reputational risks alongside new revenue streams. We’ll now examine how these alleged data-privacy...
